tasks.title: Group C - Benchmark Runner & Accuracy Gate
tasks.description: Build the benchmark runner, reporting surface, and CI accuracy gate that validate ScanMeNow detection against independent PHI/PII testing data. Task C consumes Task D extraction/OCR support, Task G PII capability, and Task H independent testing data.

tasks.title: Group D - Filesystem Walker & Integration
tasks.description: Local filesystem walker with path resolution, skip patterns, symlink policy, max-depth limits, and end-to-end CLI integration. File format scope: TXT, CSV, JSON, PDF (pdfminer.six), DOCX/XLSX/PPTX (python-docx/openpyxl/python-pptx), legacy DOC (LibreOffice headless subprocess), PNG/JPG/JPEG/GIF (pytesseract + Tesseract OCR). System dependencies: Tesseract OCR, LibreOffice. Platform scope: Windows and Linux.
tasks.notes: Hardening concern carried from Task #3 dual review (Claude Sonnet 4.6 + Codex GPT-5, 2026-05-26): when the filesystem walker pipes findings through the detection layer, expand the recognizer collision tests from 'at least one entity fires' into expected entity/span precedence behaviour. A numeric token can match MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER, ACCOUNT_NUMBER, and DEVICE_IDENTIFIER simultaneously; if downstream redaction or reporting logic needs deterministic entity precedence over overlapping PHI spans, that dedup/priority contract must be defined and tested here rather than left implicit. OCR SCOPE ADDITION (2026-05-27): Task D now includes OCR capability for image-format files (PNG, JPG, JPEG, GIF) via Tesseract (pytesseract), and legacy .doc files via LibreOffice headless subprocess (cross-platform, no extra pip package). LibreOffice is a system dependency like Tesseract; absence triggers WARNING + skip rather than crash. Linux testing will be performed on WSL. INSTALLER NOTE: Task #12 (Dependency Bootstrapper) owns auto-detection and installation of Tesseract and LibreOffice for end users.

tasks.title: Group F - Phase 1 Exit Gate & Verification
tasks.description: Accuracy threshold validation, CLI functional tests on real fixture directory, documentation, and Phase 1 completion (1 subtask)

tasks.title: Group E - Cloud & Retention Services
tasks.description: Firestore connection layer with environment flag for local dev, retention policy evaluation, and cloud integration (4 subtasks)

tasks.title: Task G - Add PII to Scope and Capability
tasks.description: Add PII scanning to the project scope and detection capability alongside PHI scanning. Task G defines the expanded PII entity set that Task C must benchmark and Task H must represent in independent testing data.

tasks.title: Task H - Independent Testing Data for Task C
tasks.description: Produce the independent synthetic PHI/PII testing corpus, companion extracted text files, golden findings JSON, near-miss decoys, and delivery notes that Task C will consume for validation, accuracy benchmarking, and regression guarding. This task must preserve independence by avoiding ScanMeNow source code, tests, and existing fixtures while authoring the corpus.
tasks.notes: PII ENTITY LABEL SET (from Task G, 2026-05-27): Task H corpus must include representative examples for all 8 US PII entity types activated by Task G: CREDIT_CARD (Luhn-validated card numbers), US_BANK_NUMBER (US routing/account numbers), US_PASSPORT (US passport numbers), US_DRIVER_LICENSE (US state driver's license numbers), IBAN_CODE (SWIFT/IBAN format bank account numbers), CRYPTO (cryptocurrency wallet addresses — Bitcoin, Ethereum, etc.), MEDICAL_LICENSE (medical license and NPI numbers), NRP (nationality/religion/political group mentions). These supplement the 15 PHI HIPAA Safe Harbor entity types already in scope. Corpus must include at least 2 positive examples and 1 near-miss decoy per PII entity type across the document set.

tasks.title: Build Bi-Temporal Progress Tracking
tasks.description: Add append-only task history so backlog changes can be queried by both project-effective time and system-recorded time while preserving docs/backlog.json as the current-state projection.

tasks.title: Dependency Bootstrapper - First-Run System Dependency Installer
tasks.description: On first Electron app launch, detect whether required system dependencies are installed (Tesseract OCR, LibreOffice headless) and silently install any that are missing. Separate install flows for Windows and Linux. Covers: version detection, silent download/install, progress feedback to the user, graceful failure with actionable error message if install is blocked (e.g. no internet, insufficient permissions). Must handle partial installs and re-check on subsequent launches. Option to bundle installers within the Electron package vs. stream from CDN on first run — decision affects package size vs. install reliability.
tasks.notes: System dependencies introduced by Task #5: Tesseract OCR (image scanning), LibreOffice headless (legacy .doc extraction). Both are system-level binaries, not pip packages. Windows install paths: Tesseract at C:\Program Files\Tesseract-OCR\tesseract.exe (winget: UB-Mannheim.TesseractOCR), LibreOffice at C:\Program Files\LibreOffice\program\soffice.exe. Linux install: apt-get install tesseract-ocr libreoffice (or equivalent). WSL-aware: distinguish WSL from native Linux for path resolution.

tasks.title: Windows Installer Package
tasks.description: Build a standalone Windows installer (.exe or .msi) for the ScanMeNow Electron app using Electron Builder. Package includes: embedded Python runtime (PyInstaller or uv-bundled), all pip dependencies, and the dependency bootstrapper (Task #12) that detects and installs Tesseract and LibreOffice on first run. Target: Windows 10/11 64-bit. Deliverable: signed installer that passes Windows SmartScreen, installs to Program Files, adds Start Menu entry and optional desktop shortcut.
tasks.notes: Electron Builder target: nsis (NSIS installer) or msi. Python embedding strategy TBD: PyInstaller bundled alongside Electron, or subprocess bridge to system Python. Code signing required for SmartScreen clearance. Consider auto-update via electron-updater.

tasks.title: Linux Installer Package
tasks.description: Build standalone Linux installer packages for the ScanMeNow Electron app. Targets: DEB (Ubuntu/Debian), RPM (RHEL/CentOS/Fedora), and AppImage (distribution-agnostic). Package includes: embedded Python runtime, all pip dependencies, and the dependency bootstrapper (Task #12) that detects and installs Tesseract and LibreOffice via the system package manager (apt, dnf, yum). Primary test environment: WSL (Ubuntu). Deliverable: three package artifacts and an install script.
tasks.notes: Electron Builder targets: deb, rpm, AppImage. WSL testing confirmed by Scott (2026-05-27). System package manager detection: prefer apt, fallback to dnf/yum/zypper. Bootstrapper must handle headless Linux installs (no GUI sudo prompt). Desktop entry (.desktop file) for GUI environments.

tasks.title: VMware VM Deployment Package
tasks.description: Build a pre-configured Linux VM image in OVF/OVA format with ScanMeNow fully installed and all system dependencies (Tesseract, LibreOffice) pre-configured. Targeted at enterprise deployments where application isolation, air-gapped environments, or IT-managed VM provisioning is required. Base OS: Ubuntu LTS (latest stable). Deliverable: OVA file importable into VMware vSphere, VMware Workstation, and VMware Fusion. Includes first-boot configuration script for network, hostname, and initial admin credentials.
tasks.notes: Base image: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (minimal server). VM spec: 4 vCPU, 8GB RAM, 60GB disk (minimum). VMware Tools pre-installed. ScanMeNow installed via Linux DEB package (Task #14 artifact). Consider also producing a VirtualBox OVA variant. Air-gap support: all dependency installers pre-bundled, no internet required post-deploy.

tasks.title: International PII Scope Expansion
tasks.description: Extend PII detection coverage beyond US-focused entity types to include international and GDPR-scoped identifiers. Activate Presidio's country-specific built-in recognizers: UK NHS number, AU TFN/Medicare/ABN/ACN, SG NRIC/FIN, EU fiscal codes (IT, ES, FI, PL), and any others supported by Presidio at implementation time. Produce a companion pii_international_coverage.json registry documenting each identifier, jurisdiction, recognizer source, and confidence notes. Depends on Task #9 (US PII foundation) being production.
tasks.notes: Deferred from Task #9 scope decision (2026-05-27): Scott confirmed US-only PII scope for Task #9. International expansion added as a separate backlog item for future consideration. No custom recognizers expected — all international types are Presidio built-ins requiring only registration.
